[[chapter_cmd]]
== CMD - Command and Control

The Command (CMD) group covers the block-split instruction forms (typed `BSTART.*`, `BSTOP`), template blocks (`FENTRY`, `FEXIT`, `FRET.*`), memory-block operations (`MCOPY`, `MSET`), cross-block calls (`XB`), and the header-descriptor instructions (`B.IOT`, `B.IOR`, `B.ARG`, `B.DIM`, `B.TEXT`, `B.HINT`, `B.CATR`, `B.DATR`) that carry per-block metadata for accelerator execution.

**Block split instructions** (`BSTART.*`, `BSTOP`): these are the canonical forms for block-structured control flow. The typed `BSTART.<type>` forms select the execution model:

* `.STD` / `.SYS` / `.FP` -- scalar execution.
* `.MPAR` / `.MSEQ` -- SIMT parallel/sequential shader kernel launch.
* `.VPAR` / `.VSEQ` -- tile-parallel/sequential vector execution (TVEC-equivalent).
* `.TMA` (with aliases `TLOAD`, `TSTORE`, `TMOV`) -- tile memory access.
* `.CUBE` (with aliases `TMATMUL`, `TMATMUL.ACC`, `ACCCVT`) -- tile matrix multiply.
* `.TEPL` -- generic template-op accelerator.
* `.FIXP` -- fixed-point block type.

Block-start aliases are canonical spelling in documentation and tests; the encoded contract is always the base selector (`BSTART.TMA`, `BSTART.CUBE`, etc.) with the function/code field carrying the operation.

**Header descriptors**: tile and scalar block metadata is conveyed by descriptor instructions that must appear immediately after a block-start marker. Descriptors are consumed in program order and do not affect the scalar execution path unless the platform profile specifies otherwise.
